memes.label: Andrew Trimmer
memes.description: The old butler at Hazlehurst Manor who has served the Treverton family for over thirty years and receives a small annuity in Jasper Treverton's will.

memes.label: "Beechampton"
memes.description: "Beechampton is the market town adjacent to Hazlehurst in Devonshire, home of the Clare family — Vicar Theodore Clare, his son Edward, and his daughter Celia — and a key social hub in the world of the novel."

memes.label: "Camelot Church"
memes.description: "Camelot Church is the remote parish church on the edge of Dartmoor where John Treverton and Laura Malcolm are married in secret — the site of the novel's 'ill-omened wedding' that sets the stage for the disaster to follow."

memes.label: "Celia Clare"
memes.description: Celia Clare is a secondary character in The Cloven Foot — the vicar's vivacious daughter, Laura Malcolm's closest friend and confidante, Edward Clare's sister, and the eventual betrothed of surgeon George Gerard

memes.label: "Cibber Street"
memes.description: "Cibber Street, in the Leicester Square district of London, is where the Chicots take lodgings — and the site of La Chicot's murder, the central crime of the novel."

memes.label: Class and Status
memes.description: The social hierarchies and class boundaries that structure the world of The Cloven Foot — inherited rank vs. earned wealth, the ambiguous figure of the gentleman, the theater demimonde vs. rural gentry, and the servant class whose knowledge threatens respectable surfaces

memes.label: "The Cloven Foot (Motif)"
memes.description: The governing motif of hidden evil beneath respectable surfaces in The Cloven Foot — the diabolical trace that betrays the concealed self

memes.label: "Desrolles"
memes.description: "Desrolles — also known as Mansfield and Malcolm — is the villain of The Cloven Foot, a dissolute lodger at Cibber Street with multiple aliases, La Chicot's secret lover, and the murderer whose exposure in the final chapters resolves the central mystery"

memes.label: Fate and Destiny
memes.description: The pervasive theme of fate as an inescapable force in The Cloven Foot, manifesting through the "cloven foot" motif, predetermined outcomes, and characters' resigned submission to their destinies.

memes.label: George Gerard
memes.description: A young surgeon and friend of Edward Clare, who attends La Chicot after the stabbing and later plays a key role in uncovering the truth about John Treverton's past.

memes.label: "Greed"
memes.description: "Financial greed as a criminal motive and moral theme in The Cloven Foot"

memes.label: "Hazlehurst Manor"
memes.description: "Hazlehurst Manor (also called Treverton Manor) is the ancestral estate of the Treverton family in Devonshire, the central setting of The Cloven Foot — a Queen Anne mansion under the shadow of Dartmoor where the novel's inheritance plot, key character relationships, and final resolution unfold."

memes.label: Identity and Disguise
memes.description: The central motif of concealed and mistaken identity in The Cloven Foot, driving the plot through John Treverton's dual existence as Jack Chicot and the mystery of Desrolles's true nature.

memes.label: Identity Deception
memes.description: The pervasive theme of concealed, assumed, and dual identity in The Cloven Foot — through alias, disguise, and the suppression of past selves

memes.label: Inheritance
memes.description: Inheritance and disinheritance as the financial engine of the plot in The Cloven Foot — Jasper Treverton's conditional will, the fourteen-thousand-a-year estate, and the threat of losing it all

memes.label: Jack Chicot
memes.description: A good-tempered but languid man, husband to La Chicot and tenant of the Cibber Street lodging-house, whose failed theatrical career and troubled marriage form part of the novel's backstory.

memes.label: Jasper Treverton
memes.description: The wealthy, eccentric landowner whose death and controversial will set the entire plot of The Cloven Foot in motion, disinheriting his only living relative in favor of his adopted daughter.

memes.label: "Jealousy"
memes.description: "Romantic and professional jealousy as a driving emotional force in The Cloven Foot — from Edward Clare's rejected-suitor malice to La Chicot's possessive fury"

memes.label: "John Treverton"
memes.description: John Treverton, also known as Jack Chicot, is the protagonist of The Cloven Foot — heir to the Treverton fortune, former army captain, artist in exile, husband of the murdered La Chicot, and the man suspected of her killing before his eventual exoneration

memes.label: Joseph Lemuel
memes.description: A wealthy London stockbroker who takes an interest in La Chicot and is described by Desrolles as "one of the wealthiest men in London."

memes.label: "Justice"
memes.description: "Justice and its pursuit as the narrative resolution of The Cloven Foot — from crime through investigation to exoneration"

memes.label: "La Chicot"
memes.description: "La Chicot (Zaïre Chicot) is the murder victim in The Cloven Foot — a former Parisian ballet dancer, wife of Jack Chicot (John Treverton), and star of the Royal Prince Frederick Theatre in London, whose violent death sets in motion the central mystery of the novel."

relations.label: Royal Prince Frederick Theatre and Music Hall

memes.label: Laura Malcolm
memes.description: The adopted daughter of Jasper Treverton and the central female figure of The Cloven Foot, whose inheritance, romantic choices, and moral discernment drive the novel's emotional and thematic core.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The urban setting of The Cloven Foot, a city of lodging-houses, law courts, and concealed identities, whose anonymity and institutional density provide the necessary conditions for the novel's plot.

memes.label: "Marriage Market"
memes.description: "Marriage as financial transaction in The Cloven Foot — the conditional will's demand that John Treverton wed Laura Malcolm within a year, the mercenary language of courtship, and the novel's critique of Victorian matrimonial economics"

memes.label: Mr. Clare
memes.description: The vicar of the parish near Camelot, co-trustee of Jasper Treverton's will, and a figure of moral authority who becomes entangled in the Treverton family's secrets.

memes.label: Mrs. Evitt
memes.description: The landlady of the Cibber Street lodging-house where La Chicot and Jack Chicot reside, and a key witness to the discovery of La Chicot's body after the stabbing.

memes.label: Mrs. Rawber
memes.description: A theatrical lodger at Mrs. Evitt's Cibber Street boarding-house who plays leading business at the Shakespeare Theatre and is an eyewitness to the aftermath of the Cibber Street murder.

memes.label: Mrs. Trimmer
memes.description: The cook and housekeeper at Hazlehurst Manor, wife of the butler Andrew Trimmer, and a loyal family retainer who has served the Trevertons for nearly as long as her husband.

memes.label: Murder
memes.description: Murder as the central crime and narrative engine in The Cloven Foot — the killing of La Chicot and its investigation

memes.label: Reputation and Scandal
memes.description: The twin forces of reputation and scandal in The Cloven Foot — how Victorian respectability functions as both social currency and weapon, and how the Chicot murder scandal becomes a destructive engine of public judgment

memes.label: Scotland Yard
memes.description: Scotland Yard is the London Metropolitan Police force in The Cloven Foot — the institutional arm of criminal investigation that pursues the murderer of La Chicot and arrests John Treverton on suspicion of murder

memes.label: Sensation Fiction
memes.description: "The Victorian sensation novel as a literary genre and its exemplary embodiment in M.E. Braddon's The Cloven Foot — crime at the heart of domesticity, hidden identities, scandal, and the press"

memes.label: The Arrest and Escape
memes.description: The dramatic arrest and subsequent escape sequence in The Cloven Foot, a pivotal event that brings the machinery of law into direct confrontation with the novel's central mystery.

memes.label: The Clare Family
memes.description: The clerical family of Beechampton in The Cloven Foot, whose members — the Vicar Mr. Clare and his daughters — serve as moral witnesses, confidantes, and occasional comic relief throughout the novel.

memes.label: The Coroner's Court
memes.description: The Coroner's Court is the legal body that conducts the inquest into the murder of La Chicot in The Cloven Foot — a formal inquiry that convenes at the Prince of Wales's Feathers public-house and adjourns without reaching a verdict, leaving the case unresolved

memes.label: The Discovery of Desrolles's Identity
memes.description: The climactic revelation event in The Cloven Foot where the true identity of the enigmatic Desrolles is exposed, unraveling the novel's central mystery.

memes.label: "The Hunt for Desrolles"
memes.description: "The climactic pursuit of the murderer Desrolles from London to Paris, where an English detective tracks him to his squalid lodgings in the Students' Quarter, confronts him, and witnesses his violent death — falling through a rotted wall during a desperate struggle"

memes.label: "The Ill-Omened Wedding"
memes.description: "The ill-omened wedding of John Treverton and Laura Malcolm at Hazlehurst Church on New Year's Eve — a marriage celebrated under gloomy skies and shadowed by the groom's secret past, followed hours later by his desertion of his bride and a cryptic letter of explanation"

relations.label: marriage as a legal and economic institution

memes.label: "The Inquest into La Chicot's Murder"
memes.description: "The coroner's inquest into the murder of La Chicot, held at the Prince of Wales's Feathers public house in Cibber Street — the formal legal inquiry that establishes Jack Chicot as the prime suspect and sets in motion the hunt for the missing husband"
aliases: Inquest at the Prince of Wales's Feathers

memes.label: "The Murder of La Chicot"
memes.description: "The violent murder of the dancer La Chicot in her bed at No. 14 Cibber Street, Leicester Square — the central crime of The Cloven Foot that transforms the novel from domestic drama into sensation mystery"

memes.label: The Press
memes.description: The Press is the newspaper industry in The Cloven Foot — the sensationalist engine that transforms the murder of La Chicot into a public spectacle and brands Jack Chicot as a murderer before any legal verdict

memes.label: "The Prince Frederick Theatre"
memes.description: "The Royal Prince Frederick Theatre and Music Hall is the London theatre where La Chicot becomes a star as premiere danseuse — the flashy music hall whose posters plaster London and whose patrons include the mysterious men who shape the novel's intrigue."
aliases: Royal Prince Frederick Theatre and Music Hall

memes.label: The Prince of Wales's Feathers
memes.description: The Prince of Wales's Feathers is the public-house at the corner of Cibber Street and Woodpecker Court in The Cloven Foot where the coroner's inquest into the murder of La Chicot is held

memes.label: "The Reading of Jasper Treverton's Will"
memes.description: "The formal reading of Jasper Treverton's last will and testament at Hazlehurst Manor, which bequeaths the Treverton estate to John Treverton on condition that he marry Laura Malcolm within one year — the event that sets the novel's central conflict in motion"

memes.label: The Servants
memes.description: The network of domestic servants in The Cloven Foot, whose access to the private lives of their employers makes them crucial witnesses, information carriers, and occasionally agents in the novel's central mystery.

memes.label: The Royal Prince Frederick Theatre
memes.description: The Royal Prince Frederick Theatre and Music Hall is the London theatre managed by Mr. Smolendo where La Chicot performs as premiere danseuse — the institution at the centre of the novel's theatrical Bohemian world
aliases: Royal Prince Frederick Theatre and Music Hall

memes.label: The Village of Beechampton
memes.description: The rural village setting in The Cloven Foot, home to the Treverton estate and the Clare family, representing the world of tradition, community, and settled identity against which the novel's urban mysteries are measured.

memes.label: Theodore Clare
memes.description: The vicar of the parish near Hazlehurst Manor, father of Edward and Celia Clare, and co-trustee of Jasper Treverton's will who receives a bequest of family plate.

memes.label: Thomas Sampson
memes.description: A young London solicitor who serves as one of Jasper Treverton's trustees and witnesses the reading of the will that sets the novel's central plot in motion.
